backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor defining all core types (Asset, RepairTicket, Part, AuditEntry, AppUser, Client) and state maps, with helper functions for CRUD operations, audit logging, and authorization via a mixin; uses explicit migration on upgrade.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module that transforms the old RepairTicket schema (missing `currentStage`) to the new schema by adding a default `currentStage` of 'Unknown', preserving all other actor state fields.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (TanStack Query hooks for repairs-by-stage and top-failing-models, and corresponding Recharts dashboard charts) to their source files, components, and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries of every frontend source file, describing fixes and additions made to hooks, pages, and components across the React frontend.
frontend/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS entry point defining CSS custom property design tokens for light and dark themes including colors, borders, chart palette, and sidebar variables.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell for the React SPA, mounting the app at `#root` and loading the Vite entry module `src/main.tsx`.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React application file that sets up TanStack Router with authentication gating via Internet Identity, profile setup modal, theme provider, and all page routes under a shared layout.
frontend/src/components/AddAssetForm.tsx: A dialog form component for registering a new asset, collecting serial number, model, client, status, and condition fields, then submitting via the useAddAsset mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/AddPartForm.tsx: A dialog form component for adding a new inventory part, collecting part number, name, compatible model, stock quantity, and low-stock threshold, then submitting via the useAddPart mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/AssetDetailModal: A modal dialog that displays detailed information about a selected asset, including its metadata fields and a full repair history table fetched by serial number.
frontend/src/components/EditAssetForm.ts: A dialog form component for editing an existing asset's model, client, status, and condition fields, pre-populated with current values and submitted via the useUpdateAsset mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: The top navigation bar component displaying the app logo/title, a theme toggle button, the authenticated user's name and role, and a logout button.
frontend/src/components/KPICard.tsx: A reusable card component for displaying a single KPI metric with a title, value, icon, optional description, and accent color variants, including a loading skeleton state.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: The main application layout component that composes the Sidebar, Header, and a main content area rendering either passed children or the router's Outlet.
frontend/src/components/NewRepairTicketF: A dialog form component for creating new repair tickets, allowing users to select an asset, enter fault details, assign a technician, set outcome, and add parts replaced.
frontend/src/components/PartsReplacedEdi: An inline editor component for managing a list of replaced parts, supporting adding, removing, and updating part selections and quantities from an available parts list.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: A modal dialog shown on first use that prompts the user to enter their name and select a role to create their app profile.
frontend/src/components/RepairsByStageCh: A bar chart component that visualizes the count of repair tickets grouped by their current stage, using Recharts and data from the useRepairsByStage hook.
frontend/src/components/RepairTicketDeta: A dialog modal that displays full details of a repair ticket and allows authorized users to edit diagnosis, outcome, and parts replaced fields.
frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx: The application sidebar with role-based navigation links, user profile display, and a branding footer.
frontend/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx: A small badge component that renders a styled label for either an AssetStatus or RepairOutcome enum value with color-coded styling.
frontend/src/components/StockAdjustmentM: A modal dialog component that allows users to add or remove stock quantity for a given part, using a mutation hook and displaying success/error toasts.
frontend/src/components/TopFailingModels: A horizontal bar chart component that displays repair counts for the top failing terminal models using Recharts, with a loading skeleton state.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A hook that creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using React Query, initializing access control and invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing DFINITY Internet Identity authentication, including login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes all React Query hooks for CRUD operations on assets, repair tickets, parts, users, clients, and audit entries by wrapping the ICP backend actor calls.
frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.ts: Provides a ThemeProvider and useTheme hook that persist and toggle between dark and light themes using localStorage and a CSS class on the document root.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines global CSS custom properties for light and dark themes (colors, borders, sidebar), imports fonts, and applies Tailwind base/component/utility layers along with custom scrollbar styling.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrappers around the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/AssetsPage.tsx: Displays a filterable, searchable table of all assets with actions to add, edit, view, delete, and export to CSV. Admin-only delete is supported, with status and client filter dropdowns.
frontend/src/pages/AuditLogPage.tsx: Shows a paginated, searchable table of audit log entries sorted by timestamp. Supports filtering by entity ID, type, description, and changed-by fields.
frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx: Renders an overview dashboard with KPI cards for assets, repairs, and low-stock parts, plus charts for repairs by stage and top failing models, and recent repair/asset lists.
frontend/src/pages/PartsInventoryPage.ts: Lists all spare parts in a table with stock levels and status, shows a low-stock alert banner, and provides modals to add parts or adjust stock quantities.
frontend/src/pages/RepairTicketsPage.tsx: Displays a filterable, searchable table of repair tickets with outcome and client filters, and supports creating new tickets, viewing details, and exporting to CSV.
frontend/src/pages/ReportsPage.tsx: Displays analytics dashboards including top repaired assets, most replaced parts, technician workload, and yearly repair limit alerts using Recharts bar charts and custom visualizations.
frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx: Admin-only settings page for managing users (add, edit role) and clients (add, rename, delete) via modal dialogs, restricted to users with the admin role.
frontend/src/utils/csvExport.ts: Provides utility functions to export assets and repair tickets as CSV files, handling value escaping and triggering browser downloads.
frontend/src/utils/formatters.ts: Contains helper functions for formatting timestamps, converting enum values (AssetStatus, RepairOutcome, AppUserRole) to display labels and CSS class names, and bigint/date conversions.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Provides utilities for reading URL query parameters from both regular and hash-based routing, persisting them to sessionStorage, and cleaning sensitive params from the URL bar.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining a design system with OKLCH CSS variable-based color tokens, custom fonts, border radii, accordion animations, and the animate and typography plugins.
project_state.json: Tracks the current project state (version 3) for a repair management app with a dashboard featuring live bar charts, dark/light mode, and admin login. Records existing features, architectural notes, known issues, and requirement mappings.
